title: Senior AI Engineer
company_focus:
Healthcare intelligence analytics and data-driven decision-
making
description:
Company Overview:

Leading with our core values of Quality, Integrity, and
Opportunity, MedInsight is one of the healthcare industry’s
most trusted solutions for healthcare intelligence. Our
company purpose is to empower easy, data-driven decision-
making on important healthcare questions. Through our
products, education, and services, MedInsight is making an
impact on healthcare by helping to drive better outcomes for
patients while reducing waste. Over 300 leading healthcare
organizations have come to rely on MedInsight analytic
solutions for healthcare cost and care management.
MedInsight has been ranked #1 for Payer Quality Analytics by
clients for the last three years in the Best in KLAS report.

MedInsight is a subsidiary of Milliman; a global, employee-
owned consultancy providing actuarial consulting, retirement
funding and healthcare financing, enterprise risk management
and regulatory compliance, data analytics and business
transformation as well as a range of other consulting and
technology solutions.

Position Summary:

We’re seeking a Senior AI Engineer with deep expertise in
healthcare claims and machine learning to design, build, and
deploy production-grade AI systems. You’ll work across
platform engineering, data science, product, and clinical
teams to create solutions that improve healthcare quality,
risk adjustment, and population health insights. This role
blends traditional ML, MLOps, and generative AI (Gen AI) to
drive impactful analytics products.

What You’ll Work On:

- MIKE (MedInsight Knowledge Engine): AI-assistant for
  healthcare analytics

- Data Confidence Model (DCM): AI-enhanced data validation and
  quality assurance

- Risk Adjustment AI: HCC recapture, risk scoring automation
  and predictive modeling

- Claims Analytics AI: Pattern recognition, fraud detection,
  and utilization forecasting

- Population Health Models: Member segmentation, risk
  stratification, and predictive analytics

- Healthcare NLP: Clinical coding, documentation improvement,
  and insights extraction
- Lakehouse AI Integration: Embedding AI across our healthcare
  data platform

Primary Responsibilities:

- Design, build, and deploy production ML models and MLOps
  pipelines
- Engineer feature-rich solutions for healthcare claims, risk
  prediction, and population health
- Implement scalable inference systems with proper validation,
  monitoring, and performance tracking
- Strategically integrate LLMs and Gen AI into analytics
  products, building RAG systems and conversational
  interfaces
- Collaborate with engineering, product, and clinical teams to
  translate business needs into AI solutions
- Ensure models meet clinical accuracy, regulatory compliance,
  and business credibility
- Contribute to ML strategy, tooling, and best practices
  across teams

Requirements:

Machine Learning & AI Expertise

- 5+ years of ML engineering experience with production
  deployments
- Strong Python skills; familiarity with scikit-learn,
  XGBoost, PyTorch, TensorFlow
- Experience with supervised/unsupervised learning, feature
  engineering, model evaluation, and MLOps
- Cloud ML platform experience (Azure ML, Databricks,
  SageMaker) and containerization (Docker/Kubernetes)
- Knowledge of generative AI, LLMs, prompt engineering, and
  RAG systems

Healthcare Analytics Experience

- 3+ years working with healthcare claims data (medical,
  pharmacy, eligibility)
- Experience with population health analytics, risk adjustment
  (CMS-HCC, HHS-HCC), and quality measures
- Familiarity with actuarial forecasting, cost trending, and
  healthcare operations

Collaboration & Communication

- Strong cross-functional collaboration skills, able to work
  with engineers, product managers, and clinical experts
- Able to translate technical concepts for non-technical
  stakeholders
- Ownership mentality: seeing initiatives from concept to
  production

Preferred Qualifications

- Advanced degree (MS/PhD) in Computer Science, Statistics,
  Applied Math, or related field
- Experience with healthcare analytics platforms (MedInsight,
  HealthEdge, Cotiviti)
- Knowledge of HEDIS, Star Ratings, and quality improvement
  programs
- Clinical NLP or medical coding experience
- HIPAA compliance and healthcare data security knowledge

Technical Environment

- Languages: Python, SQL, PySpark
- ML Engineering: scikit-learn, XGBoost, PyTorch, TensorFlow,
  MLflow, Optuna
- Gen AI: LangChain, OpenAI API, Azure OpenAI, HuggingFace,
  vector databases
- Data Platforms: Azure Databricks, Azure Data Lake,
  PostgreSQL, Delta Lake
- Cloud & MLOps: Azure ML, Azure DevOps, Docker, Kubernetes,
  CI/CD pipelines
- Analytics: PowerBI, Jupyter, VS Code, Git

Location

This role can be located remotely within the United States.
The expected application deadline for this job is February
28, 2026.

Compensation

The overall salary range for this role is $104,900 -
$199,065.

For candidates residing in:

- Alaska, California, Connecticut, Illinois, Maryland,
  Massachusetts, New Jersey, New York City,

Pennsylvania, Virginia, Washington, or the District of
Columbia the salary range is $120,635 - $199,065.

- All other locations the salary range is $104,900 - 173,100.

A combination of factors will be considered, including, but
not limited to, education, relevant work experience,
qualifications, skills, certifications, etc.

What makes this a great opportunity?

- Work for a company that values your wellbeing and
  professional growth, offering a flexible work environment,
  generous benefits package, and investment in the
  development of your career
- Join an innovative, high growth company with a solid
  industry track record and opportunities to grow your
  position within the organization
- Bring your expertise and ideas to directly impact and help
  build the next generation of MedInsight products and
  solutions

Benefits

We offer a comprehensive benefits package designed to
support employees’ health, financial security, and well-
being. Benefits include:

- Medical, Dental and Vision – Coverage for employees,
  dependents, and domestic partners.
- Employee Assistance Program (EAP) – Confidential support for
  personal and work-related challenges.
- 401(k) Plan – Includes a company matching program and
  profit-sharing contributions.
- Discretionary Bonus Program – Recognizing employee
  contributions.
- Flexible Spending Accounts (FSA) – Pre-tax savings for
  dependent care, transportation, and eligible medical
  expenses.
- Paid Time Off (PTO) – Begins accruing on the first day of
  work. Full-time employees accrue 15 days per year, and
  employees working less than full-time accrue PTO on a
  prorated basis.
- Holidays – A minimum of 10 paid holidays per year.
- Family Building Benefits – Includes adoption and fertility
  assistance.
- Paid Parental Leave – Up to 12 weeks of paid leave for
  employees who meet eligibility criteria.
- Life Insurance & AD&D – 100% of premiums covered by
  Milliman.
- Short-Term and Long-Term Disability – Fully paid by
  Milliman.

Equal Opportunity

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for
employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex,
sexual orientation, national origin, disability, or status
as a protected veteran.

ai_classification_reasoning:
This is an AI/ML engineering role that involves hands-on
work building and deploying ML models, implementing RAG
systems, and integrating LLMs into production. The role
requires direct model engineering work including feature
engineering, inference systems, and GenAI implementation,
not just supporting infrastructure.
